req.title: Inter-facility referral (boundary)
req.statement: A clinician shall send a referral to another facility; the referral shall trigger a secure message and consent check. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) a happy-path referral submission by a credentialed clinician that must be stored and trigger a secure message, (2) a consent-absent scenario that must return 403, and (3) boundary values on the timestamp and numeric fields (e.g. priority score at min/max edges). EP drives the happy-path partition selection; NEG drives the consent-absent path; BVA drives timestamp and numeric boundary probing; STC traces the referral lifecycle state machine (Draft→Active→Completed); DT covers the consent×role decision matrix.

tests.name: test_referral_happy_path_stores_and_dispatches_message
preconditions: Clinician is authenticated with role:clinician JWT
Target facility exists in the system
Patient has active consent on record
actions: Build a valid ReferralRequest payload with all required fields
POST /ReferralRequest with clinician Authorization header
Capture the HTTP response and Location header
Poll the secure-message queue for the dispatched notification
expected: GET /ReferralRequest/{id} returns status 200 with stored record
Secure message queue contains exactly one message referencing the referral id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite/api"

@pytest.fixture
def clinician_headers():
    return <fixture:clinician_auth_headers>

@pytest.fixture
def valid_referral_payload():
    return <fixture:valid_referral_payload_with_consent>

def test_referral_happy_path_stores_and_dispatches_message(
    clinician_headers, valid_referral_payload
):
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/ReferralRequest",
        json=valid_referral_payload,
        headers=clinician_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}"
    resource_id = resp.json()["id"]
    assert resource_id, "Resource id must be non-empty"

    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/ReferralRequest/{resource_id}",
        headers=clinician_headers,
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["id"] == resource_id

    messages = <helper_fn:poll_secure_message_queue>(referral_id=resource_id)
    assert len(messages) == 1, "Exactly one secure message must be dispatched"
    assert messages[0]["referralId"] == resource_id

tests.name: test_referral_returns_403_when_consent_absent
preconditions: Clinician is authenticated with role:clinician JWT
Target facility exists in the system
Patient consent record is explicitly absent or revoked
actions: Build a ReferralRequest payload referencing a patient with no consent
POST /ReferralRequest with clinician Authorization header
Capture the HTTP response status and error body
expected: Response body contains error code CONSENT_REQUIRED or equivalent
trace: R-HLT-042: error pathway 403 when consent absent
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite/api"

@pytest.fixture
def clinician_headers():
    return <fixture:clinician_auth_headers>

@pytest.fixture
def no_consent_referral_payload():
    return <fixture:referral_payload_no_consent>

def test_referral_returns_403_when_consent_absent(
    clinician_headers, no_consent_referral_payload
):
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/ReferralRequest",
        json=no_consent_referral_payload,
        headers=clinician_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "CONSENT_REQUIRED" in str(body), "Error body must cite consent requirement"

    patient_id = no_consent_referral_payload["subject"]["reference"]
    stored = <helper_fn:query_referrals_by_patient>(patient_id, clinician_headers)
    assert stored == [], "No referral must be persisted on consent failure"

    messages = <helper_fn:poll_secure_message_queue>(patient_id=patient_id)
    assert messages == [], "No secure message must be dispatched on consent failure"

tests.name: test_referral_boundary_timestamp_max_and_min_priority
preconditions: Clinician is authenticated with role:clinician JWT
Patient has active consent on record
actions: Submit referral with timestamp at maximum valid ISO-8601 date (9999-12-31T23:59:59Z)
Assert acceptance or documented rejection with correct status
Submit referral with priority score at minimum boundary value (0)
Submit referral with priority score at maximum boundary value (100)
Submit referral with priority score one below minimum (-1) and one above maximum (101)
expected: Timestamp 9999-12-31T23:59:59Z: HTTP 201 or 422 with validation detail
Priority score -1: HTTP 422 with field-level validation error
Priority score 101: HTTP 422 with field-level validation error
trace: R-HLT-042: boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements

tests.name: test_referral_state_transition_draft_to_active_to_completed
preconditions: Clinician is authenticated with role:clinician JWT
Patient has active consent on record
Referral workflow engine supports Draft, Active, Completed states
actions: POST /ReferralRequest with status=draft
PATCH /ReferralRequest/{id} to transition status to active
PATCH /ReferralRequest/{id} to transition status to completed
Attempt invalid transition from completed back to draft
trace: R-HLT-042: secure message dispatched on activation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite/api"

@pytest.fixture
def clinician_headers():
    return <fixture:clinician_auth_headers>

@pytest.fixture
def draft_referral_payload():
    return <fixture:referral_payload_status_draft>

def test_referral_state_transition_draft_to_active_to_completed(
    clinician_headers, draft_referral_payload
):
    create_resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/ReferralRequest",
        json=draft_referral_payload,
        headers=clinician_headers,
    )
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201
    rid = create_resp.json()["id"]
    assert create_resp.json()["status"] == "draft"

    active_resp = requests.patch(
        f"{BASE_URL}/ReferralRequest/{rid}",
        json={"status": "active"},
        headers=clinician_headers,
    )
    assert active_resp.status_code == 200
    assert active_resp.json()["status"] == "active"

    complete_resp = requests.patch(
        f"{BASE_URL}/ReferralRequest/{rid}",
        json={"status": "completed"},
        headers=clinician_headers,
    )
    assert complete_resp.status_code == 200
    assert complete_resp.json()["status"] == "completed"

    rollback_resp = requests.patch(
        f"{BASE_URL}/ReferralRequest/{rid}",
        json={"status": "draft"},
        headers=clinician_headers,
    )
    assert rollback_resp.status_code == 409, "Reverse transition must be rejected"

tests.name: test_referral_consent_role_decision_table
preconditions: Multiple actor/consent combinations are pre-configured in the test environment
role:nurse and role:admin tokens are available alongside role:clinician
actions: Attempt POST /ReferralRequest as role:nurse with consent present
Attempt POST /ReferralRequest as role:admin with consent present
Attempt POST /ReferralRequest as role:clinician with consent present
Attempt POST /ReferralRequest as role:clinician with consent absent
trace: R-HLT-042: error pathway 403 when consent absent
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite/api"

@pytest.mark.parametrize("headers_fixture,consent,expected_status", [
    ("nurse_headers",     True,  403),
    ("admin_headers",     True,  403),
    ("clinician_headers", True,  201),
    ("clinician_headers", False, 403),
])
def test_referral_consent_role_decision_table(
    request, headers_fixture, consent, expected_status
):
    headers = request.getfixturevalue(<fixture:role_headers_map>[headers_fixture])
    payload = (
        <fixture:valid_referral_payload_with_consent>
        if consent
        else <fixture:referral_payload_no_consent>
    )
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/ReferralRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == expected_status, (
        f"role={headers_fixture}, consent={consent}: "
        f"expected {expected_status}, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
